title: Gold - Wikipedia
content: # Gold. **Gold** is a chemical element; its chemical symbol is **Au** (from Latin *aurum*) and atomic number 79. In its pure form, it is a bright-metallic-yellow, dense, soft, malleable, and ductile metal. Chemically, gold is a transition metal, a group 11 element, and one of the noble metals. It is one of the least reactive "Reactivity (chemistry)") chemical elements, being the second lowest in the reactivity series, with only platinum ranked as less reactive. | Gold in the periodic table | |.

title: What is the chemical symbol for gold? - Quora
content: The chemical symbol for gold is Au from its Latin origin : Aurum. Gold is a chemical element with an atomic number 79.

title: What is the Element Symbol for Gold?
content: * **What is the Element Symbol for Gold?**. # What is the Element Symbol for Gold? The element symbol for gold is "AU," derived from the Latin word "aurum," meaning "shining dawn." Gold is a chemical element with the atomic number 79 and is classified as a transition metal. Known for its bright, slightly orange-yellow color, gold is dense, soft, malleable, and ductile, making it highly prized for its beauty, scarcity, and versatility. 1. **1941–1945 Mercury Dime 5-pc Set**. This unique 1916–1945 Mercury Dime Twenty-Piece Roll is a great way to start your own Mercury Dime set. title: Gold | Au (Element) - PubChem
